body:
## Summary
- Let daily summaries default to AGENT_PROJECT_MANAGEMENT_DISCUSSION_CATEGORY before falling back to General
- Keep manual workflow overrides by leaving the discussion_category input blank by default
- Document the shared category variable and add workflow regression coverage

## Verification
- npm --prefix .agent test

comments.body:
The live diff is small enough to score directly. I also ran the local `.agent` test command, but the local checkout is not on the PR head, so I’m treating the PR diff itself, not that local test run, as the evidence for the changed assertions.## Rubrics Review

| Total Score | Verdict | Rubrics Scored |
| -- | -- | -- |
| 100 | PASS | 4 |

| Dimension | Rubric | Result | Score | Evidence |
| -- | -- | -- | -- | -- |
| coding_workflow / generic | Keep docs in sync | pass | 7/7 | Updates `.agent/docs/customization/configuration-list.md` so `AGENT_PROJECT_MANAGEMENT_DISCUSSION_CATEGORY` is documented as shared by Daily Summary creation and project-management summary comments. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Reuse existing code | pass | 8/8 | Keeps the behavior in the existing `agent-daily-summary.yml` input/env path and extends the existing workflow regression test in `.agent/src/__tests__/envelope.test.ts`; no new helper or duplicated workflow logic. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Make surgical changes | pass | 8/8 | Live PR diff touches only three focused files: the daily summary workflow, one docs row, and one existing regression test. |
| coding_style / generic | Prefer simple implementations | pass | 7/7 | Implements the fallback directly as `inputs.discussion_category || vars.AGENT_PROJECT_MANAGEMENT_DISCUSSION_CATEGORY || 'General'` and leaves manual override behavior clear by making the input default blank. |

## Notes

- Main score drivers: the PR is narrow, keeps the workflow thin, documents the shared variable, and adds targeted regression assertions for both the blank manual default and variable fallback.
- No rubric-specific follow-up needed.

## Findings

- **INFO:** No rubric-specific issues found.

## Final Rubric Verdict

PASS

## AI Review Synthesis

<!-- sepo-agent-review-synthesis -->

> Dual-agent review by **Claude** and **Codex**.
> Requested by @lolipopshock.

## Summary of PR/Issue
- PR #50 makes daily summary discussion creation use `AGENT_PROJECT_MANAGEMENT_DISCUSSION_CATEGORY` when the manual workflow input is left blank.
- It keeps explicit manual overrides intact by changing the `discussion_category` workflow input default from `General` to an empty string.
- It documents that the project-management discussion category variable is shared by daily summary creation and summary comments.
- It adds workflow regression coverage for the blank default and fallback expression.

## Review
Both independent reviews found the PR narrow, documented, and covered by targeted tests. No blocking or warning issues were identified.

| Issue | Severity | Description |
| -- | -- | -- |
| No actionable issues found | INFO | Reviewers agreed the workflow fallback, docs update, and regression assertion satisfy the intended behavior. |

## Progress
- Rubrics review already passed with no required follow-up.
- Code review reported `npm --prefix .agent test` passed with 396 tests.
- Existing PR discussion has no unresolved inline review comments.
- No new inline comments were posted because there were no concrete file-line findings.

## Recommended Next Step
NO_AUTOMATED_ACTION: no unresolved actionable branch changes remain.

## Final Verdict
SHIP

## Action Items
- [ ] No required branch-change action items.

## Rubrics Update

No changes were committed to `agent/rubrics` from PR #50.

no rubric changes

PR #50 had no trusted human review comments or inline discussion. The bot feedback matched existing active rubrics for docs sync, simple implementation, surgical changes, and code reuse, so there was nothing new or durable to add. Rubrics checkout is clean.

title: Use project-management discussion category for daily summaries
